templates.title: Document Word/Char/Line Counter
templates.description: Statistik teks: kata, karakter, baris, paragraf, kalimat. Seperti wc tapi lebih lengkap.
templates.code:
def {{nama_fungsi}}(text):
    """Count text statistics: chars, words, lines, paragraphs, sentences."""
    chars = len(text)
    chars_no_space = len(text.replace(' ', '').replace('\n', '').replace('\t', ''))
    words = len(text.split())
    lines = text.count('\n') + (1 if text and not text.endswith('\n') else 0)
    paragraphs = len([p for p in text.split('\n\n') if p.strip()]) if text.strip() else 0
    sentences = len([s for s in re.split(r'[.!?]+', text) if s.strip()])
    return {
        'characters': chars,
        'characters_no_spaces': chars_no_space,
        'words': words,
        'lines': lines,
        'paragraphs': paragraphs,
        'sentences': sentences,
    }


def {{nama_fungsi_file}}(filepath, encoding='utf-8'):
    """Count statistics from a text file."""
    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding=encoding) as f:
        text = f.read()
    return {{nama_fungsi}}(text)

templates.title: Markdown → Plain Text
templates.description: Strip markdown formatting dari teks. Headers, bold, italic, links, code → plain text.
templates.code:
def {{nama_fungsi}}(markdown_text):
    """Convert Markdown to plain text. Strips formatting.
    Handles: headers, bold, italic, links, images, code, lists, blockquotes.
    """
    text = markdown_text
    # Images: ![alt](url) → alt
    text = re.sub(r'!\[([^\]]*)\]\([^\)]*\)', r'\1', text)
    # Links: [text](url) → text
    text = re.sub(r'\[([^\]]*)\]\([^\)]*\)', r'\1', text)
    # Headers: ### Title → Title
    text = re.sub(r'^#{1,6}\s+', '', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    # Bold: **text** or __text__ → text
    text = re.sub(r'\*\*(.+?)\*\*', r'\1', text)
    text = re.sub(r'__(.+?)__', r'\1', text)
    # Italic: *text* or _text_ → text
    text = re.sub(r'\*(.+?)\*', r'\1', text)
    text = re.sub(r'(?<!\w)_(.+?)_(?!\w)', r'\1', text)
    # Inline code: `code` → code
    text = re.sub(r'`(.+?)`', r'\1', text)
    # Code blocks: ```code``` → code
    text = re.sub(r'```[\s\S]*?```', '', text)
    # Blockquotes: > text → text
    text = re.sub(r'^>\s?', '', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    # List markers: - item or 1. item → item
    text = re.sub(r'^[\s]*[-*+]\s+', '', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    text = re.sub(r'^[\s]*\d+\.\s+', '', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    # Horizontal rules
    text = re.sub(r'^[-*_]{3,}\s*$', '', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    # Collapse multiple blank lines
    text = re.sub(r'\n{3,}', '\n\n', text)
    return text.strip()

templates.title: Simple Keyword Extractor (TF-based)
templates.description: Ekstrak kata kunci dari teks berdasarkan Term Frequency. Tanpa library eksternal.
templates.code:
STOP_WORDS = {'the', 'and', 'for', 'are', 'but', 'not', 'you', 'all', 'can', 'had', 'her', 'was', 'one', 'our', 'out', 'has', 'have', 'been', 'this', 'that', 'with', 'from', 'they', 'will', 'what', 'when', 'where', 'which', 'their', 'there', 'about', 'would', 'could', 'should', 'also', 'into', 'more', 'some', 'than', 'other', 'these', 'just', 'over', 'such', 'take', 'each', 'very', 'may', 'its', 'only', 'known', 'after', 'certain'}


def {{nama_fungsi}}(text, top_n={{default_top_n}}, min_length={{min_word_length}}, stop_words=None):
    """Extract top keywords from text based on term frequency.
    Returns list of (word, count) tuples sorted by frequency.
    """
    stops = stop_words if stop_words is not None else STOP_WORDS
    words = re.findall(r'\b[a-zA-Z]+\b', text.lower())
    filtered = [w for w in words if len(w) >= min_length and w not in stops]
    freq = collections.Counter(filtered)
    return freq.most_common(top_n)


def extract_keyphrases(text, ngram_size=2, top_n=10):
    """Extract top keyphrases (n-grams) from text.
    Returns list of (phrase, count) tuples.
    """
    words = re.findall(r'\b[a-zA-Z]+\b', text.lower())
    filtered = [w for w in words if w not in STOP_WORDS]
    ngrams = collections.Counter()
    for i in range(len(filtered) - ngram_size + 1):
        phrase = ' '.join(filtered[i:i + ngram_size])
        ngrams[phrase] += 1
    return ngrams.most_common(top_n)

templates.title: Text Summarizer (Extractive)
templates.description: Ringkas teks dengan memilih kalimat paling penting berdasarkan TF. Stdlib only.
templates.code:
def {{nama_fungsi}}(text, num_sentences=None, ratio={{default_ratio}}):
    """Extractive text summarizer using sentence scoring.
    Scores sentences by word frequency (TF-based).
    num_sentences: fixed number of sentences to keep.
    ratio: fraction of original sentences to keep (0.0-1.0).
    Returns summarized text.
    """
    sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', text.strip())
    if len(sentences) <= 2:
        return text.strip()
    # Word frequencies
    words = re.findall(r'\b[a-zA-Z]+\b', text.lower())
    stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'is', 'are', 'was', 'were', 'be', 'been', 'being',
            'have', 'has', 'had', 'do', 'does', 'did', 'will', 'would', 'could',
            'should', 'may', 'might', 'can', 'to', 'of', 'in', 'for', 'on', 'with',
            'at', 'by', 'from', 'as', 'into', 'through', 'during', 'and', 'but',
            'or', 'not', 'no', 'if', 'it', 'its', 'this', 'that'}
    freq = collections.Counter(w for w in words if w not in stop and len(w) > 2)
    # Score sentences
    scored = []
    for i, sent in enumerate(sentences):
        sent_words = re.findall(r'\b[a-zA-Z]+\b', sent.lower())
        score = sum(freq.get(w, 0) for w in sent_words)
        # Normalize by sentence length
        score = score / max(len(sent_words), 1)
        # Slight boost for early sentences
        if i < 3:
            score *= 1.2
        scored.append((i, score, sent))
    # Select top sentences
    if num_sentences is None:
        num_sentences = max(1, int(len(sentences) * float(ratio)))
    top = sorted(scored, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:num_sentences]
    # Restore original order
    top.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
    return ' '.join(s[2] for s in top)

templates.title: Readability Score — Flesch-Kincaid
templates.description: Hitung skor keterbacaan teks: Flesch Reading Ease, Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level, Gunning Fog.
templates.code:
def {{nama_fungsi}}(text):
    """Calculate readability scores for English text.
    Returns dict with: flesch_reading_ease, flesch_kincaid_grade, gunning_fog,
    word_count, sentence_count, syllable_count.
    """
    sentences = [s.strip() for s in re.split(r'[.!?]+', text) if s.strip()]
    words = re.findall(r'[a-zA-Z]+', text)
    num_sentences = max(len(sentences), 1)
    num_words = max(len(words), 1)
    num_syllables = sum(_count_syllables(w) for w in words)
    # Flesch Reading Ease
    fre = 206.835 - 1.015 * (num_words / num_sentences) - 84.6 * (num_syllables / num_words)
    # Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level
    fkgl = 0.39 * (num_words / num_sentences) + 11.8 * (num_syllables / num_words) - 15.59
    # Gunning Fog Index
    complex_words = sum(1 for w in words if _count_syllables(w) >= 3)
    fog = 0.4 * ((num_words / num_sentences) + 100 * (complex_words / num_words))
    return {
        'flesch_reading_ease': round(fre, 2),
        'flesch_kincaid_grade': round(fkgl, 2),
        'gunning_fog': round(fog, 2),
        'word_count': num_words,
        'sentence_count': num_sentences,
        'avg_words_per_sentence': round(num_words / num_sentences, 2),
        'avg_syllables_per_word': round(num_syllables / num_words, 2),
    }


def _count_syllables(word):
    """Estimate syllable count for an English word."""
    word = word.lower()
    if len(word) <= 3:
        return 1
    word = re.sub(r'(?:[^laeiouy]es|ed|[^laeiouy]e)$', '', word)
    word = re.sub(r'^y', '', word)
    vowels = re.findall(r'[aeiouy]+', word)
    return max(len(vowels), 1)
